BASED ON PICTURES AND VIDEOS RECEIVED FROM TRAINED SPOTTERS, LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA, AND THE PUBLIC, NWS MELBOURNE CAN CONFIRM THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTH-CENTRAL SAINT LUCIE COUNTY THIS AFTERNOON. PEAK WINDS ARE ESTIMATED AROUND 65-70MPH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN APPROXIMATELY 7 MILES WSW OF LAKEWOOD PARK, IN A FIELD NORTH OF THE FLORIDA TURNPIKE AND WEST OF INTERSTATE 95, AT APPROXIMATELY 606 PM EDT. THE TORNADO TRAVELED SLOWLY TO THE SOUTHEAST, EVENTUALLY CROSSING THE TURNPIKE A

A NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED MINOR STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE FROM A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM IN A RESIDENTIAL COMMUNITY ADJACENT TO THE INDIAN RIVER GOLF CLUB, CENTERED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 19TH PL SW & 8TH AVE SW IN VERO BEACH. THE SURVEY NOTED TWO UPROOTED TREES AS WELL AS DAMAGE TO STREET SIGNS AND FENCING. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 60 MPH. THIS SEVERE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED AN EF-0 TORNADO SEVERAL MINUTES LATER IN THE NEARBY MIDWAY ESTATES MOBILE HOME COMMUNITY.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE MELBOURNE DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMS THAT A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE MIDWAY ESTATES MOBILE HOME COMMUNITY IN VERO BEACH. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO THE EAST-SOUTHEAST FOR APPROXIMATELY 4 MINUTES ALONG A 0.5 MILE LONG TRACK UNTIL IT DISSAPATED AS IT CROSSED INTO THE VERO SHORES RESIDENTIAL COMMUNITY. THE TORNADO HAD A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED BETWEEN 75-85 MPH. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WILL BE AVAILABLE IN FORTHCOMING PNS.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER ESTIMATES WIND GUSTS TO 50KT/58MPH NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY A1A AND DRIFTWOOD DR. IN VERO BEACH.
